heading: Create a Materialized View
body:
- For the cluster that contains the sample data,
  click Browse Collections.

- In the left navigation pane, select the
  sample\_airbnb database.

- Select the listingsAndReviews collection.

The [$project](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/project) stage modifies data in the
`listingsAndReviews` collection to convert existing fields into
`string` type fields.

- Click Add Stage.

- Select [$project](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/project) from the Select
  drop-down menu.

- Add the following syntax to the aggregation pipeline editor. This does the following:

  - Converts the date objects in the `last_scraped` field
    into string objects in the `YYYY-MM-DD` format using
    [$dateToString](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/dateToString) and writes the output to a string
    field called `lastScrapedDate`.

  - Converts the number objects in the `accommodates` and
    `minimum_nights` fields into string values using
    [$toString](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/toString) and writes the output to number fields
    called `accommodatesNumber` and `minimumNumberOfNights`,
    respectively.

  - Includes the `name` and `property_type` string fields in
    the output and renames them to `propertyName` and
    `propertyType` respectively.

  ```json
  {
    lastScrapedDate: { $dateToString: { format: "%Y-%m-%d", date: "$last_scraped" } },
    accommodatesNumber: { $toString: "$accommodates" },
    maximumNumberOfNights: { $toString: "$maximum_nights" },
    propertyName: "$name",
    propertyType: "$property_type"
  }
  ```

The [$merge](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge) writes the output fields from the
[$project](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/project) stage to a materialized view named
`airbnb_mat_view` in the `sample_airbnb` database.

- Click Add Stage.

- Select the [$merge](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge) stage from the Select
  drop-down menu.

- Add the following syntax to the aggregation pipeline editor:

  ```json
  {
    into: "airbnb_mat_view",
    whenMatched: "replace"
  }
  ```

Refresh the list of collections to see the `airbnb_mat_view` collection.

To learn how to query the `airbnb_mat_view` collection in the
Atlas UI, see [View, Filter, and Sort Documents](atlas/atlas-ui/documents).

codeBlocks.content:
{
  lastScrapedDate: { $dateToString: { format: "%Y-%m-%d", date: "$last_scraped" } },
  accommodatesNumber: { $toString: "$accommodates" },
  maximumNumberOfNights: { $toString: "$maximum_nights" },
  propertyName: "$name",
  propertyType: "$property_type"
}
